item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man i sure do hate it when I'm sleeping and bugs come out of my pillow, fuck inside my body and then go back inside.

+1 for that mental image alone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an okay concept that is dragged down by unnecessary addenda. Novote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this, sorry. It feels to similar to SCP-743. It doesn't do enough to stand out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FUCK THIS FUCKING ARTICLE AND FUCK READING IT LYING DOWN!

+1

item.title: Reminiscent
item.content:encoded: Dang, I remember when I gave some critique on this… what was it, years ago? So happy to see it in the new Series, and it turned out great. A definite upvote from me and thanks for the credit. It always feels cool to have assisted in writing a successful SCP. It's very reminiscent of Series I or II style writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm on the fence about this one - it's just a Thing That Does a Thing, but the thing it does is creepy, and it does it well. Five years ago I'd have upvoted this immediately, but I've gotten spoiled since then. I'm no-voting for now, but I'm sure I'll be thinking about this later, which means I'll probably come back and upvote it.

EDIT: yep, you've got me side-eyeing my pillow. Harmless or not, I don't want arthropods mating inside me. Successfully inducing paranoia about everyday objects takes me back to the good old days, so have a +1.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I hate bugs, and so I found this creepy enough to record. Then the author requested it after it was finished, so that was a happy coincidence! Hope you enjoy it!

https://youtu.be/ppj_bUZfOGU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually love this. It really feels like an older Series 1 article, and it does leave me uncomfortable after reading it, even now. A +1 for you, for such good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this. I think I'd be okay with the insects using humans to reproduce if it actually caused anomalous effects later on down the line (i.e. everyone who sleeps on the SCP slowly turns into a human beehive, or something).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I was lying down while reading it, and now feel distinctly uncomfortable.

It's the kind of horror parodied in one of the -Js (the thing on the back of your head), but done here absolutely seriously. A nicely vague and creepy object.

+1

item.title: Re: Shouldn't it be classified "Safe"
item.content:encoded: If we factor in the first addendum, the pillow appears to be actively trying to escape from containment. Hence, I think that justifies Euclid.

item.title: Shouldn't it be classified "Safe"
item.content:encoded: It can be kept in the glass box with no active dependencies to satisfy. Since it isn't required to "actively" look after it, this classifies it as safe. Euclid is something that requires attention from foundation staff, perhaps maintenance of containment or killing instances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have to say that I disagree. While the two SCPs are 'insect,' emitting animals, the variation with this one is much greater, though the premise is (kind of,) the same I will agree, but the pillow doesn't have the need to feed the same as the chocolate fountain. This one was pretty OK, no vote as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I went and took the time to read the whole thing, and this honestly feels like a pillow version of SCP-743. To be honest though, despite this, I don't mind the idea. It's a shame that this isn't quite good enough to upvote. If (or when) this article moves into deletion; I urge you to keep this idea, or let someone rewrite it.

Overall, I'm not going to upvote or downvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still hold onto my initial critique regarding the first addendum. It begins with a claim that is not suggested of prior. You are better off focusing solely on the actions, and let readers deduce that the pillow is somehow sentient. Stating it from the get-go, in this case, ruins the entire addendum.

Also, I still fail to see why the insects need human hosts to reproduce. It feels like a contrived attempt to generate insect horror.

item.title: Authors Note
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to Captain Raptin, Leveritas, J13586, Shaggydredlocks, Doctor Cicely Lander, BitVyper, Lex1nat0r and MrWrong for feedback!

I've been working on this for a while, and final worked up the courage to post to the main list. Hope you enjoyed!

EDIT: I don't know why I haven't done this yet, but Site-42's TheeSherm did an audio adaptation of this article!

So, here it the Official Audio Adaptation of SCP-3232
